text:
French pole vaulter Anthony Ammirati's Olympic gold medal dreams came crashing down after his bulge brought the bar crashing down.

A French pole vaulter’s Olympic gold medal dreams have gone up in smoke after part of his crotch hit the bar on his third attempt at clearing the 5.70m height.

Anthony Ammirati, 21, caught the attention of eagle-eyed social media users overnight who noticed just what part of the French pole vaulter’s body saw him knocked out of the games.

Viral footage circulating X, formerly Twitter, showed the Frenchman’s member catching the bar on his way down, bringing his gold medal hopes with him and great amusement to quippy commenters.

Anthony Ammirati failed the bar and the commentators are clearly having a hard time acknowledging what happened ðŸ˜‚ HELP I'M DYINGpic.twitter.com/5hOHttVA5g

“Oh, he brought that baguette to the games,” quipped one user.

Another added: “Suffering from success.”

One said: “Well, you win some, you lose some.”

Though social media users saw the humorous side to the pole vaulter's demise, Ammirati was devastated, appearing visibly upset after being knocked out.

He wound up finishing 12th in Group A after clearing 5.60 metres, while fellow French competitors Robin Emig and Thibaut Collet were also left out of the qualifying round, recording heights of 5.60m and 5.75m respectively.

More quips about the Frenchman’s downfall came after a touching joint post to Instagram congratulating Ammirati on his Olympic performance.

But the social media users were not quite so sympathetic to his loss as they were ready to make more jibes about his unfortunate exit.

“We loved seeing his HUGE effort!” one jokingly wrote.

One quipped: “Girls are all here for the baguette?”

Another said: “It’s been a schlong road to get here, and we are all so proud of you.”

Ammirati is not the first pole vaulter to be eliminated by his bulge as Japanese competitor Hiroki Ogita knocked the bar off with a sensitive part of his body in the 2016 Rio de Janeiro games.

The men’s pole vaulting final will take place at 3am on Tuesday morning (Australian Eastern time).

Aussie pole vaulter Kurtis Marschall has passed through to the final after he cleared the dreaded 5.70m bar on Saturday.
